name: Inspect dataset structure before writing import code
description: Tests Rule 1 (Inspect Before Importing) and Rule 5 (Report Data Summary). The agent should refuse to dive straight into hard-coded imports and instead enumerate the dataset's structure, identify available sensors, check for annotations, and present a summary before proceeding.
prompt: I have a driving dataset at C:/datasets/my_drive_001/. Please import it into Scenario Builder so I can build a scenario.

assertions.description: Response inspects directory structure before writing import code (uses dir/ls/listing or asks the user about contents)
assertions.expected: directory listing, dir(), or explicit clarifying question about dataset structure

assertions.description: Response identifies sensor modalities (GPS, lidar, camera) and checks for pre-computed annotations rather than assuming they don't exist
assertions.expected: mentions checking for object_detection.json / labels/ / annotations/ / tracking/ folders

assertions.description: Response presents a data summary template (Dataset, Recording, Duration, Available sensors, Calibration) before importing
assertions.expected: summary block listing sensors and their formats/rates

assertions.description: Response does NOT immediately call scenariobuilder.GPSData() or run a lidar tracker on day one without inspecting the dataset first
assertions.expected: no premature scenariobuilder.GPSData(...) construction in the very first code block

name: Use existing annotations instead of running a lidar tracker
description: Tests Rule 2 (Check for Existing Annotations Before Computing Tracks). When per-frame 3D bounding boxes with track IDs already exist, the agent must map them directly to ActorTrackData and NOT run a clustering or PointPillars pipeline.
prompt: My dataset at C:/datasets/clip_42/ has a folder annotations/ with per-frame 3D bounding box JSON files (each object has class, location_3d, size, orientation, track_id). I also have lidar in lidar/*.pcd. I need ActorTrackData for the actors. What's the best approach?

assertions.description: Response maps the existing per-frame annotations to ActorTrackData (timestamps, trackIDs, positions cell arrays)
assertions.expected: scenariobuilder.ActorTrackData(timestamps, trackIDs, positions)

assertions.description: Response does NOT recommend running a lidar tracker, PointPillars, or Euclidean clustering when annotations exist
assertions.expected: no pointPillarsObjectDetector, pcsegdist, segmentLidarData, JPDA tracker recommendation as the primary path

assertions.description: Response asks about or verifies the coordinate frame of the annotations (ego/world/sensor) before using them
assertions.expected: mention of ego frame vs world frame, or explicit clarifying question

assertions.description: Generated mapping code is syntactically valid MATLAB and would execute given valid inputs
assertions.expected: code parses cleanly via checkcode and runs with synthetic placeholder data

name: Zero GPS altitude when targeting OpenStreetMap roads
description: Tests the altitude-handling rule under Step 1 of the import pipeline: OSM has no elevation data, so altitude must be zeroed before constructing GPSData when the user plans to use OSM roads. This is a non-obvious gotcha an agent would miss without the skill.
prompt: I have GPS data from my drive: timestamps (Nx1), latitude (Nx1), longitude (Nx1), altitude (Nx1) measured by a GNSS unit with ~5 m vertical noise. I'm going to fetch the road network from OpenStreetMap. Construct the GPSData object.

assertions.description: Response zeros the altitude (altitude = zeros(...)) before constructing GPSData because OSM roads have no elevation
assertions.expected: altitude = zeros(size(timestamps)) or equivalent, with explanation referencing OSM

assertions.description: Response uses the scenariobuilder.GPSData constructor with the four positional arguments in the documented order
assertions.expected: scenariobuilder.GPSData(timestamps

assertions.description: Generated code constructs a valid GPSData object from synthetic inputs without error
assertions.expected: code runs end-to-end and class(gpsData) == 'scenariobuilder.GPSData'

assertions.description: Response calls convertTimestamps and normalizeTimestamps on the GPSData after construction (the documented downstream steps)
assertions.expected: convertTimestamps(gpsData, ...) and normalizeTimestamps(gpsData)

name: Multi-sensor inspection routes through scenariobuilder.* + drivingLogAnalyzer
description: Tests the canonical DLA inspection path. When a user says 'visualize this multi-sensor dataset' (camera + lidar + GPS + actor tracks all present), the agent must wrap each sensor into the matching scenariobuilder.* object so they sit in the base workspace, then launch drivingLogAnalyzer (bare — the app takes no programmatic args; user imports via GUI Import → From Workspace) — NOT roll its own VideoWriter dashboard. Radar / IMU stay as raw structs (no wrapper exists). Per-frame 3D detections without persistent track IDs are CONVERTED to scenariobuilder.ActorTrackData with placeholder per-frame IDs and made available for the DLA Actors overlay — which projects the cuboids natively when CameraData has Intrinsics + Mounting attached.
prompt: Visualize the multi-sensor data in C:/Users/adeshmuk/OneDrive - MathWorks/devai/sbskills/scenario_builder/scenario-builder-getting-started/VSILabs_Seq001. The dataset has GPS, camera, lidar, and actor tracks. Inspect it interactively.

assertions.description: Wraps GPS data into scenariobuilder.GPSData (or via recordedSensorData factory).
assertions.expected: scenariobuilder.GPSData

assertions.description: Wraps camera data into scenariobuilder.CameraData (or via recordedSensorData factory).
assertions.expected: scenariobuilder.CameraData

assertions.description: Wraps lidar data into scenariobuilder.LidarData (or via recordedSensorData factory).
assertions.expected: scenariobuilder.LidarData

assertions.description: Launches drivingLogAnalyzer for interactive inspection (after wrapping sensors into the base workspace).
assertions.expected: drivingLogAnalyzer

assertions.description: Launches drivingLogAnalyzer in the bare form — the app takes no programmatic sensor inputs; the user imports from the workspace via the GUI. The wrapped sensors must already exist in the base workspace at launch time.
assertions.expected: drivingLogAnalyzer is called with no arguments (or trailing-arg-less). NO programmatic forms like drivingLogAnalyzer(sensors, Plot=true), drivingLogAnalyzer({gps,cam,lidar}, ...), or drivingLogAnalyzer(gpsData, Plot=true) appear — those signatures are not supported. The wrapped scenariobuilder.* objects (gpsData, cameraData, lidarData, trackData) are visible in the base workspace at the time of the launch call (e.g. via top-level script execution or assignin('base', ...)).

assertions.description: Does NOT fabricate scenariobuilder.RadarData (no such class exists).
assertions.expected: scenariobuilder.RadarData

assertions.description: Does NOT fabricate scenariobuilder.IMUData (no such class exists).
assertions.expected: scenariobuilder.IMUData

assertions.description: Does NOT build a manual VideoWriter dashboard for general 'inspect this dataset' requests — DLA is the canonical inspection path. Per-frame 3D detections (CubeRCNN-style corners3D without persistent IDs) must be CONVERTED to scenariobuilder.ActorTrackData with placeholder per-frame IDs and added to the DLA cell array; DLA's native camera Actors overlay renders the cuboids when CameraData has Intrinsics + Mounting attached. No manual world2img + VideoWriter cuboid overlay should be built.
assertions.expected: For the inspection step, the script launches drivingLogAnalyzer (bare) with the wrapped sensors already sitting in the base workspace for GUI Import → From Workspace. No multi-pane VideoWriter loop reimplementing camera/radar/lidar panes that DLA already provides. If per-frame corners3D detections are present, they are converted into scenariobuilder.ActorTrackData with placeholder per-frame IDs (e.g. det_<frameIdx>_<i>) and exposed to DLA via the base workspace — NOT rendered via a custom world2img + 12-edge cuboid VideoWriter loop.

assertions.description: Generated end-to-end script runs without error in MATLAB.

name: Per-sensor-folder + separate timestamps/*.mat schema imports correctly
description: Schema-variant guard for the Schema B import pattern. Many recordings (VSI-Labs PostProcessed, polysync, ros2bag dumps) lay out data as one folder per sensor (.jpeg / .pcd / per-sample structs) plus a sibling timestamps/<Sensor>.mat carrying the time vector. The agent must pair each sensor's binary stream with its matching timestamps file, flatten cell-array-of-structs GPS into column vectors, and build scenariobuilder.* wrappers — not assume a single consolidated sensorData.mat.
prompt: Wrap the multi-sensor data in //mathworks/WorldWide/IN-Hyderabad/devel/sarovar/02/datasets/VSI-Labs/PostProcessed/001 into scenariobuilder.* objects and launch drivingLogAnalyzer. The dataset uses per-sensor folders (ExternalRGBCamera/*.jpeg, OSLidar/*.pcd, GPS/GPSData.mat) plus a separate timestamps/ subfolder (timestamps/ExternalRGBCamera.mat, timestamps/OSLidar.mat, timestamps/GPS.mat). GPS is a cell array of per-sample structs with Latitude/Longitude/Altitude fields.

assertions.description: Loads camera image-file list from the per-sensor folder via dir(...,'*.jpeg') (or '*.png') and pairs with timestamps/ExternalRGBCamera.mat, NOT a non-existent consolidated sensorData.mat.
assertions.expected: dir(fullfile(dataDir, 'ExternalRGBCamera', '*.jpeg')) + load(fullfile(dataDir, 'timestamps', 'ExternalRGBCamera.mat')) — sorted file list and matching timestamp vector.

assertions.description: Loads lidar pcd-file list from the per-sensor folder via dir(...,'*.pcd') and pairs with timestamps/OSLidar.mat.
assertions.expected: dir(fullfile(dataDir, 'OSLidar', '*.pcd')) + load(fullfile(dataDir, 'timestamps', 'OSLidar.mat')).

assertions.description: Flattens the GPS cell-array-of-structs into Latitude/Longitude/Altitude column vectors before constructing scenariobuilder.GPSData.
assertions.expected: Loop or arrayfun over the GPSData cell array extracting .Latitude / .Longitude / .Altitude into N-by-1 numeric column vectors before scenariobuilder.GPSData(timestamps, lat, lon, alt).

assertions.description: Sorts file lists alphabetically (or by name suffix) so the i-th frame matches the i-th timestamp.
assertions.expected: sort({files.name}) (or equivalent) applied to the dir() result before pairing with the timestamp vector.

assertions.description: Builds scenariobuilder.CameraData with the file-list constructor form.
assertions.expected: scenariobuilder.CameraData

assertions.description: Builds scenariobuilder.LidarData with the pcd-file-list constructor form.
assertions.expected: scenariobuilder.LidarData

assertions.description: Hands the wrapped sensors to drivingLogAnalyzer.
assertions.expected: drivingLogAnalyzer

assertions.description: Generated end-to-end script runs without error in MATLAB.

name: Attach camera SensorParameters as a 4-field struct (DLA overlay requirement)
description: DLA's actor/lane camera-overlay refuses a bare cameraIntrinsics — it needs the full struct with MountingLocation, MountingAngles, Intrinsics, EgoOriginHeight (and EgoOriginHeight must be > 0). Putting CameraHeight into EgoOriginHeight (instead of MountingLocation(3)) lifts every actor by camHeight and they project at the horizon ('boxes in the air'). When the dataset has no intrinsics at all, the agent must leave SensorParameters empty and warn the user — never fabricate a pinhole guess.
prompt: I have an image-sequence camera (1920x1080) plus a CameraIntrinsics struct (fx=1970, fy=1970, cx=970, cy=483) and CameraHeight=1.66 m from the dataset. I have no MountingAngles or extra calibration. Wrap the camera into scenariobuilder.CameraData so DLA's actor overlay works.

assertions.description: Builds cameraData.SensorParameters as a struct with EXACTLY the four fields MountingLocation, MountingAngles, Intrinsics, EgoOriginHeight — not a bare cameraIntrinsics object.
assertions.expected: struct("MountingLocation", ..., "MountingAngles", ..., "Intrinsics", ..., "EgoOriginHeight", ...)

assertions.description: Puts the camera height in MountingLocation(3), NOT in EgoOriginHeight. Putting CameraHeight in EgoOriginHeight produces the 'boxes in the air' failure mode.
assertions.expected: MountingLocation = [forward, 0, CameraHeight] (or equivalent), with EgoOriginHeight set to a small positive scalar (e.g. 0.001), not CameraHeight.

assertions.description: EgoOriginHeight is a positive finite scalar (DLA rejects 0 with 'must be a positive and finite scalar'). Acceptable: any small positive value such as 0.001.
assertions.expected: EgoOriginHeight > 0 (e.g. 0.001), NOT 0 and NOT a negative or non-finite value.

assertions.description: Surfaces MountingAngles (especially pitch) and the chosen defaults to the user, recommending verification in DLA. Does NOT silently choose all four params and proceed without telling the user what defaults were used.
assertions.expected: Response prints the chosen MountingLocation / MountingAngles / EgoOriginHeight values and asks the user to verify the overlay in DLA, calling out pitch as the most impactful angle.

assertions.description: Generated code constructs a valid CameraData with the struct-shaped SensorParameters from synthetic inputs without error.

name: DLA is NOT auto-launched when user says 'virtualize / build scenario from this data'
description: HARD RULE guard. When the user says 'virtualize this data', 'generate a scenario from …', or 'build a scenario', the downstream target is a RoadRunner/drivingScenario export (matlab-scenario-builder skill). The driving-data-importer wraps sensors and hands off — it must NEVER auto-launch drivingLogAnalyzer as a verification or inspection step after wrapping. DLA is opt-in only: explicit user request ('open in DLA', 'drivingLogAnalyzer', 'inspect', 'visualize this recording') or a reported sensor-data problem. Auto-launching DLA pauses the user's scenario-building flow — a recurring first-attempt failure.
prompt: Virtualize this driving dataset: C:/datasets/highway_drive_01/sensorData.mat (GPS, Camera, ActorTracks). Build a scenario in RoadRunner with OpenStreetMap roads.

assertions.description: Does NOT call drivingLogAnalyzer after wrapping the sensors (auto-launch is forbidden for scenario-building requests).
assertions.expected: drivingLogAnalyzer(

assertions.description: Wraps sensors into scenariobuilder.* objects (GPSData, CameraData, ActorTrackData) and proceeds to matlab-scenario-builder skill path (trajectory, OSM, exportToRoadRunner) — NOT the inspection path.
assertions.expected: After wrapping, the script proceeds to trajectory(gpsData, ...) / getMapROI / exportToRoadRunner — the matlab-scenario-builder workflow. No DLA pause step between wrap and trajectory.

assertions.description: Does NOT call play(cameraData) as an intermediate inspection step (play is interactive DLA-like inspection).
assertions.expected: play(cameraData

name: Without source intrinsics, leave SensorParameters empty (do not fabricate)
description: When the dataset ships no intrinsics at all, the agent must leave cameraData.SensorParameters empty and tell the user DLA overlay will be disabled until they provide calibration. Fabricating a pinhole guess (e.g. fx=fy=imageWidth) produces a plausible-looking but wrong overlay, which is worse than a disabled one.
prompt: Wrap the camera in this dataset into scenariobuilder.CameraData. Source: image folder Camera/*.jpeg (1024x768) plus a CameraData table with timestamp + filename. There is NO CameraIntrinsics, NO calibration file, NO CameraHeight in the source. I want DLA actor overlay to work.

assertions.description: Leaves cameraData.SensorParameters EMPTY (does not assign anything) when source has no intrinsics, and explicitly tells the user DLA overlay will be disabled until they provide fx/fy/cx/cy from calibration.
assertions.expected: Response does NOT assign cameraData.SensorParameters, AND it states overlay is disabled / asks user for calibration values.

assertions.description: Does NOT fabricate intrinsics from image size (e.g. fx=fy=imageWidth) and present them as if they were real calibration.
assertions.expected: no fx=imageWidth / fy=imageWidth heuristic, no 'I'll use a default 60-degree FOV pinhole' substitution.

assertions.description: Generated code builds a valid CameraData (without SensorParameters) from synthetic inputs without error.
